events.event: First recorded solo with King Oliver on "Chimes Blues"
First recording as a sideman with Fletcher Henderson in New York, "Manda"
Takes influential solo on "Shanghai Shuffle" with Fletcher Henderson
First recording with Sidney Bechet, "Texas Moaner Blues"
Solo on Clarence Williams's "Everybody Loves My Baby" helps turn that side into a hit
Records "Cake Walking Babies from Home" with Sidney Bechet
Records "St. Louis Blues" with Bessie Smith
Featured on his own composition "Sugar Foot Stomp" with Fletcher Henderson
First Hot Five session — "Gut Bucket Blues" becomes a hit
Records two of his most influential pieces with the Hot Five: "Heebie Jeebies" and "Cornet Chop Suey"
Records "Stomp Off, Let's Go" with Erskine Tate's Vendome Orchestra
Records "Big Butter and Egg Man" with the Hot Five
Records "Wild Man Blues" at the first Hot Seven session
Records a famous solo on "Potato Head Blues" with the Hot Seven
Records a famous solo on "Struttin' With Some Barbecue" with the Hot Five
Records "Hotter Than That" at the final session of the original Hot Five
Records "A Monday Date" at the first session of the new Hot Five with Earl "Fatha" Hines on piano
Records perhaps his most famous solo on "West End Blues"
Records famous duet with Earl Hines on "Weather Bird"
Ends his 1920s series of Chicago recordings with "Tight Like This"
First integrated recording "Knockin' a Jug"; first pop tune "I Can't Give You Anything But Love"; and famous solo on "Mahogany Hall Stomp"
Records "Ain't Misbehavin'," which turned him into a Broadway star
Records "(What Did I Do to be so) Black and Blue," one of the first protest songs
Records popular version of "When You're Smiling"
Records his first interracial vocal duet, "Rockin' Chair" with Hoagy Carmichael
Records "Blue Yodel Number 9" with Jimmie Rodgers, the father of country music
Records a famous solo on "I'm a Ding Dong Daddy (from Dumas)"
Records a popular version of "I'm Confessin' (That I Love You)"
Fuses jazz and Cuban rhythms on "The Peanut Vendor"
Records his eventual theme song, "When It's Sleepy Time Down South," for the first time
Records popular version of "(I'll Be Glad When You're Dead) You Rascal You"
Records popular version of "All of Me"
Films "A Rhapsody in Black and Blue"
Records famous version of "That's My Home"
Records famous version of "I Gotta Right to Sing the Blues"
Films famous performances in Copenhagen, Denmark
Records famous two-part version of "On the Sunny Side of the Street"
Records first session for Decca Records, featuring "I'm in the Mood for Love"
Records "Swing That Music," named after his recently published autobiography
Films appearance in "Pennies from Heaven" with Bing Crosby
Debuts as host of "The Fleischmann's Yeast Show," a pioneering radio program
Records famous versions of "Struttin' with Some Barbecue" and "Jubilee"
Records first version of "When the Saints Go Marching In"
Records several spirituals, including "Nobody Knows the Trouble I've Seen"
Records famous version of "When It's Sleepy Time Down South"
Films "Jam Session," performing "I Can't Give You Anything But Love"
Records with Ella Fitzgerald for the first time on "You Won't Be Satisfied"
Records "Do You Know What It Means to Miss New Orleans"
Leads concert at Town Hall — particularly "Rockin' Chair"
Records live album, "Satchmo at Symphony Hall"
Records hit record of "La Vie En Rose"
Records "Dream a Little Dream of Me" with Ella Fitzgerald
Records hit record of "A Kiss to Build a Dream On"
Records hit record of "Kiss of Fire"
Records famous album "Louis Armstrong Plays W. C. Handy"
Records hit record of "Mack the Knife"
Performs "St. Louis Blues (Concerto Grosso)" with the New York Philharmonic conducted by Leonard Bernstein at Lewisohn Stadium
Records famous album "Ella and Louis Again"
Records two albums: "Louis Under the Stars" and "I've Got the World on a String"
Records "Porgy and Bess" with Ella Fitzgerald
Records "Louie and the Dukes of Dixieland"
Records two albums with Duke Ellington: "For the First Time" and "The Great Reunion"
Records "The Real Ambassadors" — a jazz musical about the role of jazz in international diplomacy
Records the original single "What a Wonderful World"
Records "We Have All the Time in the World," used in James Bond film "On Her Majesty's Secret Service"
England, Denmark, Sweden, Netherlands, France, Paris, Switzerland
"Iron Curtain" tour (Prague, East Berlin), European tour

Records "Ain't Misbehavin'," which turned him into a star on Broadway

Records hit record of "Mack the Knife"

Records famous album "Ella and Louis Again" (you can link to "Stompin' at the Savoy"

1968 - Records "Disney Songs the Satchmo Way" (you can link to "When You Wish Upon a Star")
Records the single "We Have All the Time in the World," used in James Bond film "On Her Majesty's Secret Service"
At 62, Louis Armstrong ended the Beatles’ 14-week run at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 when “Hello, Dolly!” replaced “Can’t Buy Me Love” on May 9, 1964.
Records his composition "Dipper Mouth Blues" with King Oliver.
Takes a series of improvised cornet breaks on "Tears" with King Oliver.
Records final session with King Oliver
Records three selections with blues singer Ma Rainey in New York City
Takes first vocal break on "Everybody Loves My Baby" with Fletcher Henderson
Records three selections with blues singer Maggie Jones
Records "TNT" at final New York session with Fletcher Henderson
Records "Trouble in Mind" with Bertha "Chippie" Hill
Records "King of the Zulus" with the Hot Five
Records two selections with Carroll Dickerson's Savoyagers in Chicago
Records "St. Louis Blues" with Luis Russell's Orchestra
Records "That's My Home" with Chick Webb's Orchestra for RCA Victor
Records "Basin Street Blues" for RCA Victor
Records popular version of "Old Man Mose" for Decca
Records "Pennies from Heaven" with Bing Crosby
Records first of several sessions with the Mills Brothers
Records his composition "Heart Full of Rhythm"
Records George and Ira Gershwin's "Love Walked In"
Records "Jeepers Creepers," Academy Award-nominated song from the film "Going Places"
Records the spiritual "Bye and Bye" for Decca
Records instrumental showcase for his band, "Wolverine Blues"
Reunion session with Sidney Bechet for Decca
Records "I Never Knew" at his final session before the recording ban
Records two selections with the Esquire All-American Winners, featuring Duke Ellington, Don Byas, Johnny Hodges, and Charlie Shavers
Records "Back O'Town Blues" for RCA Victor
Final recording session of Louis Armstrong and His Orchestra
RCA Victor records Armstrong's historic Town Hall concert
Records live album, "Satchmo at Symphony Hall"
Performs at the first Nice International Jazz Festival in France
Records two duets with Billie Holiday at their only studio session
Records two duets with Louis Jordan at their only studio session
Records live album for Decca, "Satchmo at Pasadena"
Records "Because of You" and "Cold Cold Heart" for Decca
Records remake of "When It's Sleepy Time Down South" for Decca
Records popular version of "It Takes Two to Tango"
Records two popular Christmas songs, "Cool Yule" and "Zat You Santa Claus"
Records parody version of "The Whiffenpoof Song"
Records two live albums for Decca, "At the Crescendo"
Sessions for popular live album "Ambassador Satch," released May 1, 1956
Records live concert "The Great Chicago Concert" for Columbia -
Records "Bing and Satchmo" LP with Bing Crosby
Records live concert at the BBC in London, later released as "Louis in London"
Records final studio album, "Louis 'Country and Western' Armstrong"
